frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: TD learning with a spectrum of discount factors rather than one

frameworks.name: temporal discounting
frameworks.one_line: exponential discounting generalized to multiple per-neuron time constants

frameworks.name: reward prediction error coding
frameworks.one_line: midbrain dopamine neurons encode RPE with heterogeneous timescales

core_question: Do dopamine neurons implement reinforcement learning at a single discount timescale or at a diversity of timescales across cells?
key_assumptions: dopamine RPE signals can be modeled within a TD-learning framework
discount factor is a stable, cell-specific property of each neuron
classical RL with a single discount factor is the baseline to generalize
manipulations: two distinct behavioral tasks performed by mice
modeled RL agents endowed with a spectrum of discount factors
key_findings: RL agents with multiple discount timescales gain distinct computational benefits
individual dopamine neurons encode RPE with heterogeneous discount time constants
the model captures both cue-evoked transients and slower dopamine ramps
each neuron's measured discount factor is correlated across the two tasks
interpretations: temporal discounting is heterogeneous and cell-specific in the dopamine system
multi-timescale RL explains diversity of transient and ramping dopamine signals
